title: Analyses of mitochondrial function in SNpc with the Seahorse XFe96
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"ea59i","text":"Protocol for analyses of mitochondrial function in SNpc with the Seahorse XFe96","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Place the punches of SNpc in a well of XFe96 Cell Culture Microplate (Agilent Technologies, CA) filled with 180 µl aCSF with 1 mM sodium pyruvate solution (Gibco‱,Grand Island, NY and Scotland, UK)
Place the XFe96 Cell Culture Microplate in a non-CO 2 incubator at 37°C for 30 minutes

Calibrate by loading each injection port (A, B, and C) of the XFe96 cartridge with: oligomycin 5 μM (SigmaAldrich, USA), FCCP (2-[2-[4- (trifluoromethoxy) phenyl] hydrazinylidene]-propanedinitrile) 1 μM (SigmaAldrich, USA) + Pyruvate 0.75 μM (Gibco‱,Grand Island, NY and Scotland, UK), or rotenone 1 µM (Sigma Aldrich USA) /antimycin A 1 µM (Sigma Aldrich, USA)
Extract the plate and insert the XFe96 Cell Culture Microplate containing the tissue punches to start the assay of mitochondrial functionality

Perform the assay of mitochondrial functionality consisting of 4 cycles of measurements (each consisting of: 2 minutes of mix, 1 minute of wait, and 3 minutes of measurement) to assess the oxygen consumption rate (OCR) before (baseline) and after injection of different drugs
creator: Ada Ledonne
